examples_of_errors: Synaptic latency units: paper writes tau_l = 1 ms which matches intended physics, but source text literally reports '1 mS' (likely a typo in the source for 1 ms) - paper made the correct interpretation.
Minor: 'simulated oscillation frequency within 5 Hz of experimental value' is derived from 50 Hz vs 55 Hz - correct arithmetic but a minor descriptive flourish not literally stated in source.

brief_notes: Strong, faithful reproduction of the source. Network architecture (N=5000, NE=4000, NI=1000, p=0.2, C=10^3), membrane parameters (Cm 0.5/0.2 nF, gm 25/20 nS, tau_rp 2/1 ms), synaptic time constants (AMPA 1/0.2/2, NMDA 1/2/100, GABA 1/0.5/5 ms), Mg parameters (beta=0.062 mV^-1, gamma=3.57 mM, [Mg2+]=1 mM), firing rates at baseline (5.2/20 steady, 5.5/21 critical) and +5% (7.8/26, 15/42), 50 Hz simulated oscillation, 58 Hz theoretical onset, ~55 Hz experimental with +-18 ms correlation peaks, +-20 ms in model, DPX timing (1000/1000/500 ms), AX 69%/31%, neuron pair counts (978/368 cue, 1246/434 response), PCP 0.25-0.30 mg/kg IM, 400 um electrodes (400-1400 um spans), dt=0.1 ms, nu_X=5 Hz, NMDA/GABA=0.2 ratio, K~200 trials, geometric mean 5 Hz threshold, PFC ~10 Hz - all match. Tables well-organized. Writing is clean with clear section flow and appropriate citations. Minor gap: no figures, and some secondary details (e.g., spatial connectivity, conductance magnitudes) are omitted since source extraction lacks them. Overall a high-fidelity, well-written manuscript.
